label: Fire Drill
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: take a photo of your fire-drill rally point — the spot outside where your class gathers. A predictable visual makes the drill enormously less scary. Pair the tile with the language you use ("we go to our spot").

label: Lockdown Drill
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: take a photo of the calm corner of your classroom where students gather during a lockdown drill. Keep the image friendly and unambiguous — this is about predictability, not alarm.

label: Field Trip
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: snap a photo of the school bus or your class lining up at the door for a field trip. Use the same photo across trips; what changes is the destination tile that follows.

label: Library Day
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of your school library entrance, or the librarian, or the rug where storytime happens. Schedule it as a recurring tile on library days.

label: Picture Day
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the photo-backdrop area, or a friendly camera icon. Surface this only on picture day so the student knows what to expect.

label: Show and Tell
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the rug, podium, or "presenter spot" your class uses. Pair with a sentence-starter strip if the student needs scaffolding.

label: PE
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the gym door or PE teacher. PE is loud and physical — a predictable visual helps an AAC user transition into it.

label: Art
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the art room or art teacher. Pair with material tiles (paint, clay, glue) the student can request.

label: Music
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the music room, music teacher, or a familiar instrument. Music is often a strong reinforcer for AAC learners — make it requestable.

label: Lunchroom
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of your cafeteria (or the table the student's class sits at). Use the same lunchroom image, then layer the menu choices as separate tiles.

label: Arrival
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the classroom entrance from the hallway side, OR the drop-off door. Helps the student name the start of the school day.

label: Dismissal
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the dismissal door, OR the bus loop, OR the line-up spot. Pair with "Bye" gestalt for an end-of-day script.

label: Centers
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of your centers rotation chart, or the rug/table where centers happen. If you can, make one tile per center (reading center, math center, etc.) and link them all to this one.

label: Line up
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the line-up spot at the door, ideally with the painted line or floor marker visible. Pair with "follow" or "go" gestalts.

label: Quiet time
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the cozy corner, rest mat, or whatever signals quiet time in your room. Pair with a "calm" tile for self-regulation language.

label: Calm corner
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the calm-down spot in your room (beanbag, sensory bin, etc.). Make the tile available as a request so the student can ask to go there.

label: My teacher
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: head-and-shoulders photo of the lead teacher. Each year this tile updates — keep the slug, swap the image.

label: My aide
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: head-and-shoulders photo of the paraprofessional/aide working with the student. Replace per year / per assignment.

label: Class pet
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of your class pet (or class mascot stuffie). Strong daily-vocabulary anchor.

label: My class job
prompt_template: SKELETON for the school team: photo of the chart spot showing this student's class job (line leader, paper passer, etc.). Update weekly with the rotation.

label: Therapy room
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of YOUR therapy room from the doorway. The single most useful transition cue for an AAC user.

label: Session start
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of your session-start routine (greeting board, name card, whatever you open with). Bracket the session in visuals so the child knows what to expect.

label: Session end
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of your end-of-session marker (timer hitting zero, "all done" card, the door). Pair with the "all done" gestalt.

label: My reinforcer
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of THIS child's strongest reinforcer (the bubble machine, their iPad video, the spinner). Make it requestable; reinforce by handing over what they asked for.

label: Sensory bin
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of the sensory bin the child engages with. If you swap the contents weekly, photo each variant as a separate tile (rice bin, bean bin, water bin) and link them.

label: Visual schedule
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of your printed/laminated visual schedule. Surface this tile when transitioning between activities — "let's check the schedule".

label: Target word
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: this is a placeholder you duplicate per session target — photo of the actual referent you're working on (a specific toy, a specific snack). Update the label per target.

label: Co-treat partner
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: head-and-shoulders photo of the OT/SLP/BCBA you co-treat with. Helps the child anchor multi-person sessions.

label: Quiet space
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of the calm corner / sensory tent / whatever de-escalation space you use. Available as a request — children CAN ask for a break.

label: Favorite toy here
prompt_template: SKELETON for the therapist: photo of the toy in YOUR room the child reliably reaches for. Different from a home favorite — this is what works in YOUR space.

label: Bedtime
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of your bedtime routine's anchor moment (bath, brush teeth, books in the rocker). Pair with the "good night" gestalt for a complete bedtime script.

label: Grandma's house
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of grandma's front door, OR grandma herself, OR the room your child plays in there. Helps the child name where they're going.

label: Grandpa's house
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of grandpa's house OR grandpa himself. Make one tile per grandparent the child sees regularly.

label: My lovey
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of THE comfort object (the specific blanket, the specific stuffie). Use the child's actual word for it as the label.

label: Our pet
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of YOUR family pet (not a stock dog/cat). Use the pet's real name as the label.

label: Our car
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of YOUR car (interior with the child's car seat visible works great). Pair with "go" + a destination tile.

label: Our park
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of the playground or park you visit most. Adds a "where" answer the child can actually request.

label: Special snack
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of THE snack — the one you keep on the top shelf, the one that's the reinforcer. Specific is far better than generic.

label: Bath time
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of your bathtub with the bath toys / bubbles your child knows. Pair with "more" and "all done" for full bath-time language.

label: Favorite book
prompt_template: SKELETON for parents: photo of the cover of THE book — the one you've read a hundred times. Pair with "read" or "again".

label: My show
prompt_template: SKELETON: photo (or screenshot) of the show your child reaches for. We do not bundle specific titles here for licensing reasons — every family fills this in with their own.

label: My movie
prompt_template: SKELETON: photo (or poster) of the movie the child requests over and over. Same idea as "my show" but for a specific movie.

label: My song
prompt_template: SKELETON: image of the album cover, the speaker, or even just a friendly music note for THE song. Pair with "more" + "again" for full requesting language.

label: My character
prompt_template: SKELETON: image of the character (toy figure photo works great). The character your child names everything after.

label: My app
